description: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to 0 and a translation vector also set to 0. Draw a new face and within this face, draw a single loop consisting of 1 circle. Center the circle at coordinates [0.375, 0.375] and set the radius to 0.375. Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust its size. Rotate the scaled sketch using the Euler angles [0, 0, 0] and translate it using the translation vector [0, 0, 0]. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.05 units to generate a new solid body. The resulting shape is a cylindrical object with a flat circular top and bottom, measuring 0.75 units in length and width, and 0.05 units in height.

Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to 0 and a translation vector also set to 0. Begin by drawing a 2-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, draw a straight line from the origin (0, 0) to the 0 (0.75, 0). Next, draw an arc starting at (0.75, 0), passing through the midpoint (0.375, 0.2126), and ending back at the origin. Scale the entire sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled sketch into 3 dimensions by rotating it with Euler angles set to 0 and translating it with a vector set to 0. Extrude the sketch 0.0214 units along the normal direction to create a new solid body. The final dimensions of the part are 0.75 units in length, 0.2126 units in width, and 0.0214 units in height.
